text: 45-year-old male with long-standing type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications. Hemoglobin A1c 8.2%. Continues metformin 1000 mg BID. Office visit, established patient, low complexity decision-making.
Patient presents with essential hypertension, well controlled on lisinopril. Blood pressure 128/78. Routine follow-up, established patient, straightforward visit.
Acute upper respiratory infection of unspecified etiology. Runny nose, sore throat, low-grade fever for 3 days. Rest and fluids advised. Brief problem-focused visit.
Asthma, unspecified, uncomplicated. Reports occasional wheeze with exercise. Albuterol inhaler prescribed PRN. Established patient, moderate complexity.
Major depressive disorder, single episode, moderate. Started sertraline 50 mg daily. Follow-up in 4 weeks. Extended office visit, moderate complexity.
Acute bronchitis with productive cough for 5 days. Lungs with scattered rhonchi. Amoxicillin prescribed. Established patient visit.
Pure hypercholesterolemia. LDL 182. Started atorvastatin 20 mg nightly. Lifestyle counseling given. Established patient, moderate complexity.
Gastro-esophageal reflux disease without esophagitis. Heartburn after meals. Omeprazole 20 mg daily started. Routine visit.
Low back pain, unspecified, of 2 weeks duration. No red-flag symptoms. NSAIDs and physical therapy recommended. Established patient.
Iron deficiency anemia secondary to chronic blood loss. Hemoglobin 9.8. Started oral iron supplementation. Follow-up labs in 6 weeks.
Urinary tract infection, site not specified. Dysuria and frequency for 3 days. Nitrofurantoin prescribed. Brief visit.
Migraine without aura, not intractable. Patient reports 2-3 episodes per month. Sumatriptan prescribed for acute treatment. Established patient.
Seasonal allergic rhinitis. Sneezing and nasal congestion every spring. Started fluticasone nasal spray. Brief follow-up.
Hypothyroidism, unspecified. TSH 8.4. Levothyroxine 50 mcg daily initiated. Follow-up TSH in 6 weeks.
Obesity, unspecified. BMI 33.4. Nutrition counseling and exercise plan discussed. Established patient, moderate complexity.
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, unspecified, with acute exacerbation. Increased dyspnea and sputum. Prednisone taper and azithromycin prescribed.
Osteoarthritis of knee, unspecified. Bilateral knee pain worse with activity. Acetaminophen and PT referral. Established patient.
Generalized anxiety disorder. Chronic worry affecting work. Started escitalopram 10 mg daily. Follow-up in 2 weeks.
Chronic kidney disease, stage 3a. eGFR 52. ACE inhibitor continued, avoid NSAIDs counseled. Moderate complexity visit.
Atrial fibrillation, unspecified. New onset irregular rhythm on exam. Apixaban 5 mg BID started, cardiology referral placed.
